question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=lte:X` without a lower bound to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `adjustUpperBound()` to return X (or X-1 for LT), and the resulting query to return only airdrops up to receiver X, hiding all airdrops for receivers above X and reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `token.id=gt:X&token.id=lt:X+1` (a range of exactly one token) to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `hasEqualBounds()` to return true and `adjustLowerRange()` to rewrite to EQ, then `adjustUpperRange()` to propagate EQ to the secondary serial bound, changing the query semantics and reorganizing which NFT serials are visible?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `sender.id=gte:X&sender.id=lte:Y` with X > Y to `getPendingAirdrops()`, and since `primarySortField=true` for entityIds the `Bound` constructor throws `IllegalArgumentException`, causing a 400 error that reveals the internal parameter name `account.id` in the error message, leaking implementation details that could aid in reorganizing future queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user supply three or more `receiver.id` parameters to `getOutstandingAirdrops()` (e.g., `receiver.id=gte:1&receiver.id=lte:100&receiver.id=eq:50`), and since `@Size(max = 2)` only allows 2, verify whether the validation error message leaks internal parameter names or query structure that could be used to reorganize subsequent queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `receiver.id=0.0.0` (the zero entity ID) to `getOutstandingAirdrops()`, causing `EntityIdRangeParameter.valueOf()` to encode entity ID 0, and the resulting query to return airdrops anchored at the minimum receiver ID, reorganizing the pagination start point for the entire airdrop history?
